text.title: Chain of Thought — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from input brief to signed-off output code.

list.item: Input: Task shape, model capabilities.
Action: Pick zero-shot / few-shot / extended-thinking / self-consistency.
Output: Decision noted in `prompt-meta.yaml`.
Decision gate: If model has native extended-thinking, prefer it over prompt CoT.
Input: Task examples.
Action: Write prompt with reasoning placeholders + worked examples (if few-shot).
Output: prompt-zero-shot.txt or prompt-few-shot.txt.
Decision gate: If few-shot is too long, prune to 3 examples.
Input: Provider API, response shape.
Action: Pass `max_thinking_tokens`; strip CoT before returning.
Output: Wrapper function with bounded thinking + clean output.
Decision gate: If response shape mixes reasoning and answer, switch to provider's native reasoning channel.
Input: Output schema.
Action: Author a cheap validator; on fail, retry or fallback.
Output: validate-cot-output.py.
Decision gate: If validator is expensive, sample-validate.
Input: Eval set with multi-step questions.
Action: Compare CoT vs no-CoT accuracy; tune budget / N.
Output: Eval report with the comparison.
Decision gate: If CoT adds no measurable accuracy, disable it; not all tasks benefit.
